Detect anomalies

N-gram anomaly detection over primary records observed in the lookback window

v2/anomaly.recent
Analysing…
How it works: Every primary record in the chosen window is fingerprinted (the record's key + json_fingerprint(data)), and the resulting strings are scored by mean n-gram rarity — lines using phrases that are uncommon across the rest of the corpus. Anything scoring at or above the threshold is flagged as an anomaly. Each flagged line carries a small novel_ngrams list explaining which phrases were rare. Use a stricter threshold (≥ 0.8) to surface only the most striking outliers; lower it (~0.5) on small corpora where every n-gram has higher base rarity.
Scanning recent primaries…
{% endblock %}
